findings_summary.title: E03_UP ranked top bottleneck but is startup-only artifact
findings_summary.detail: E03_UP queue time (6.03 min mean) reflects only the first ~20 minutes of simulation when all trucks dispatch from PARK through the ramp. After first cycle, trucks never traverse E03_UP again. True steady-state bottleneck is D_CRUSH (crusher) at 95% utilisation.

findings_summary.title: warmup_minutes config key present but not implemented
findings_summary.detail: YAML includes warmup_minutes: 0 but the runner never reads this field. All trucks start at PARK simultaneously causing startup surge. Honestly documented in model_limitations.

findings_summary.title: Redundant node_overrides in crusher_slowdown.yaml
findings_summary.detail: CRUSH service_time_mean_min override never read by simulation (uses dump_points_df instead). Harmless.

findings_summary.title: _LOAD_NODE_TO_LOADER module-level dict is dead code
findings_summary.detail: Declared in simulation.py:19 but never written to or read anywhere.

findings_summary.title: additional_scenarios_proposed is empty
findings_summary.detail: Optional per spec but represents missed analysis opportunity.

findings_summary.title: shift_length_hours patched into rep metrics externally
findings_summary.detail: aggregate_replication() doesn't include shift_length_hours; patched in by run_experiments.py. Works but inelegant.
